behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the real `git` rename signal crossing a subprocess boundary (`git` status/diff output and rename detection semantics). A mock parser test can verify local logic, but can miss real CLI-format/threshold behaviors where rename metadata is emitted differently. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: no AI/ML dependency and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI via subprocess, and assert report shows `renamed=1` with no corresponding increment in `added`/`deleted` for that file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real git subprocess output for binary changes and real filesystem binary content handling. In-process fakes can hide encoding/format edge cases that trigger crashes. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: no model output quality or agent pipeline involved.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, add/modify a binary file (e.g., random bytes), run the CLI, assert exit code is success and binary file is included in counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The failure typically appears when `git` commands against an unborn HEAD return real non-zero exit/error text. That is subprocess and repository-state dependent, so mocks can mask the real failure mode. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: no AI/ML and no multi-step agent reasoning.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a fresh git repo with no commits, run the CLI, assert successful exit and an empty structured report instead of an exception/error output.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This validates end-to-end correctness against actual repository history semantics, which come from real git operations and subprocess responses across commits. Mocked history risks always matching fixture assumptions and hiding real git behavior mismatches. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: deterministic SCM data, no AI model judgment, no chained agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history containing known adds/deletes/renames/modifications across multiple commits, run the CLI, and compare reported totals/categories to independently computed expected counts from git porcelain/plumbing commands.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real git index vs working-tree mechanics, which are OS/filesystem plus git subprocess semantics. Integration fakes for index state can miss true staged/unstaged edge behavior. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: no AI/ML output dependence and no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent file changes in two scenarios (staged vs unstaged), run the CLI in each scenario, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured output.
